description:
A customer is experiencing sporadic TLS handshake-failures.  
  Your assignment, as the support engineer on duty, is to collect a **certificate
  diagnostics bundle** from the staging server.

  1. Scan every file that ends in “.pem” **directly** inside the directory  
     /home/user/certs/input  
     ( no recursion – only the three PEM files that already exist in that exact
       path have to be processed ).

  2. Each of those PEM files is a *text* file that contains a very small mock
     certificate.  
     The information we care about is stored on four single-line key/value
     records that look exactly like this example line:

         NotAfter=2020-01-31 00:00:00

     You therefore must read the following 4 keys from every file:
       • Subject  
       • Issuer  
       • NotBefore  
       • NotAfter  

  3. For every certificate calculate  

        DAYS_VALIDITY = ( NotAfter – NotBefore )  
                        expressed as an **integer number of whole days**  
                        (24-hour periods).

     Hint: GNU date can convert a date-string to seconds since epoch; after the
     subtraction divide by 86400 and round *down* to get whole days.

  4. Produce a single, human-readable diagnostics log called

        /home/user/cert_diagnostics/cert_diagnostic_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log

     • Use the current local time to build the timestamp portion of the file
       name.  
     • Create the directory /home/user/cert_diagnostics if it does not yet
       exist.

  5. The **log file must follow this *exact* layout** – the automated grader
     performs a strict byte-comparison (except for dynamically generated
     timestamps):

        Line 1:  CERTIFICATE_DIAGNOSTICS_REPORT
        Line 2:  Generated on: <ISO-8601 timestamp, e.g. 2023-07-16T14:32:05>
        Line 3:  (blank line)
        Line 4:  FILE_PATH|SUBJECT|ISSUER|NOT_BEFORE|NOT_AFTER|DAYS_VALIDITY
        Line 5+: One line per certificate in any order you prefer, using the
                 same “|” separator and *no* surrounding spaces.
                 Example (line wrapped here for readability):
                 /home/user/certs/input/www_acme_com.pem|CN=www.acme.com|CN=Example Root CA|2021-06-01 00:00:00|2021-12-01 00:00:00|183
        After the last certificate line add one blank line.

        Summary section – three lines, exactly these labels:
        TOTAL_CERTIFICATES: <number>
        VALIDITY_GT_180_DAYS: <number of certs whose DAYS_VALIDITY > 180>
        VALIDITY_LT_30_DAYS: <number of certs whose DAYS_VALIDITY < 30>

     Apart from the timestamp, *every* character (upper-case letters, colons,
     pipes, etc.) must match the specification.

  6. On completion, leave the generated log file in place; the autograder will
     examine it to ensure:
        • File exists at the expected full path.
        • Header and summary lines match exactly.
        • A data row is present for each of the three PEM files.
        • DAYS_VALIDITY as well as the summary counters are mathematically
          correct.

  You do **NOT** have root privileges, so stay inside /home/user.  
  No extra packages need to be installed – stick to standard GNU/Linux
  user-space tools that are normally present in minimal distributions
  (bash, GNU coreutils, grep, awk, etc.).

  When the log file passes automated verification the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
     Pre-existing certificate mock-files inside the task image.  The agent
     must *NOT* see these values, but the autograder will read them to verify
     the generated report.
  -->
  <existing_files>
    <file path="/home/user/certs/input/example_root.pem"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject=CN=Example Root CA
Issuer=CN=Example Root CA
NotBefore=2020-01-01 00:00:00
NotAfter=2020-01-31 00:00:00
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/certs/input/www_acme_com.pem"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject=CN=www.acme.com
Issuer=CN=Example Root CA
NotBefore=2021-06-01 00:00:00
NotAfter=2021-12-01 00:00:00
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/certs/input/expired_test.pem"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
Subject=CN=expired.example.com
Issuer=CN=Example Root CA
NotBefore=2019-01-01 00:00:00
NotAfter=2019-01-15 00:00:00
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
]]></file>
  </existing_files>

  <expected_values>
    <cert path="/home/user/certs/input/example_root.pem">
      <days_validity>30</days_validity>
    </cert>
    <cert path="/home/user/certs/input/www_acme_com.pem">
      <days_validity>183</days_validity>
    </cert>
    <cert path="/home/user/certs/input/expired_test.pem">
      <days_validity>14</days_validity>
    </cert>

    <summary>
      <total>3</total>
      <gt_180>1</gt_180> <!-- www_acme_com.pem -->
      <lt_30>1</lt_30>  <!-- expired_test.pem -->
    </summary>
  </expected_values>

  <!--
     The grader will:
       • Compute DAYS_VALIDITY for each file and compare to the values above.
       • Examine the generated log file for:
           - Exact header lines
           - Presence of every certificate line (order not enforced)
           - Correct numeric fields
           - Correct summary block
  -->
name: task_000000_2e74f4dd
